title: dodecatheon pulchellum var. album
intro: Dodecatheon pulchellum var. album, also known as the white shooting star, is a flowering plant species within the gentian family (Gentianaceae) that is native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds, and has been used to treat various health issues such as respiratory problems and skin conditions. The health benefits of Dodecatheon pulchellum var. album include its potential to soothe coughs, reduce fever, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable resource in herbal medicine. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve making a tea from its flowers and roots, or using it as a tincture to extract its medicinal compounds.
